description:
Für mein Techniker-Projekt brauche ich ein vollständiges 3D-Modell einer mechanischen Erfindung. Sobald der Auftrag vergeben ist, stelle ich dir sämtliche Eckdaten, Skizzen und Maße zur Verfügung. 

Die Zeichnung soll direkt in AutoCAD aufgebaut werden, da ich dort alle weiteren Anpassungen und Dokumentationen vornehme. Bitte halte das Modell so aufbereitet, dass Ansichten, Schnitte und spätere Explosionsdarstellungen problemlos erzeugt werden können. 

Akzeptanzkriterien – klar und knapp: 
• native AutoCAD-Datei (.dwg) und ein neutrales Austauschformat (.step oder .iges) 
• sauber strukturierte Ebenen, eindeutig benannte Bauteile 
• korrekte Bemaßung nach den gelieferten Vorgaben ±0,1 mm 
• alle beweglichen Komponenten als separate Bodies, damit Bewegungsabläufe simuliert werden können 
- eine Stückliste der Bauteile ist ebenfalls nötig

ich werde mit meinen Schulpartnern alles bereden und dann den Auftrag am Wochenende überarbeiten und erteilen.

Wenn dir noch Informationen fehlen, melde dich kurz; ich liefere sie umgehend nach. Ich freue mich auf ein präzises, aufgeräumtes Modell, das ich direkt in meine Projektdokumentation übernehmen kann.
preview_description: Für mein Techniker-Projekt brauche ich ein vollständiges 3D-Modell einer mechanischen Erfindung. Sob
title: AutoCAD 3D-Modell Mechanik

description:
Here's an overview so you can judge whether this is a fit.

THE PROJECT (Phase 1 = a paid test sprint)

I'm launching a bootstrapped new B2B SaaS product and want to validate it through Google Ads, using a long-tail, low-competition keyword strategy: many specific, low-volume search terms (niche professional intent), each pointing to its own hyper-specific dedicated landing page. The bet is low CPC + high intent rather than broad, expensive head terms.


How the funnel works (so the goal makes sense):

- Ad → a dedicated landing page for that niche
- On the page, the visitor can try an interactive tool; if it resonates, they create a free account = a "signup"

That signup is the conversion event you'd optimize toward. (What happens after signup — paid conversion — lives in our internal system, isn't visible at ad level, and isn't reliably measurable in a short test anyway.)


TWO PARALLEL LANGUAGE LEGS, EQUAL BUDGET (so we can compare them cleanly):

- German-speaking markets (Germany, Austria, German-speaking Switzerland) — ~€1,000
- English-speaking markets — ~€1,000 — preferably UK + Ireland (closest fit for our expansion path, and cleaner auction economics than the US on these terms)

THE ONE KPI YOU'D OPTIMIZE: Cost-per-Signup

Deliberately the single target, because it bundles everything: cheap junk clicks won't produce signups, so they won't help your number — it forces low CPC and high intent at once.

Primary goal: lowest possible cost-per-signup.
Read floor (not a goal): ~25–30 signups per leg over the sprint, so the data is meaningful. Beyond that floor, lower cost-per-signup always beats more signups — this is a learning test, not a volume push.
Explicitly NOT targets: CPC alone, impressions, clicks, or raw volume above the floor.

A SECOND, EQUALLY IMPORTANT READ: is this scalable?

The €1k isn't just "how cheap is a signup" — it also has to tell me whether there's enough long-tail demand to build this into a scalable mid-term channel, or whether we hit a ceiling fast. So alongside cost-per-signup I'll want your read on:

Impression share & lost-IS (budget) per cluster — how much of available demand are we capturing at €1k?
Total available search volume vs. what we're buying — how big is the pool?
Whether cost-per-signup stays stable as spend increases (headroom) or climbs (saturation). 

I'D LIKE TO SPLIT THE WORK INTO A CLEAR SEQUENCE

WS-0 — Keyword Strategy & Pull (first, standalone):

Strategy sparring (open): given the KPI above — low cost-per-signup, high active intent, NOT raw volume — what types of keyword clusters would you go after here? I have a working hypothesis (~7 clusters across a few audiences), but I'm genuinely looking for your expertise to find high-intent, low-cost-per-signup clusters I've missed — keyword themes outside my framing are exactly what I want to surface. The only filter: sharp active intent over raw volume. This is a discussion, not just a list.
Pull & structure: Keyword Planner research across both languages (DE + EN), real search volumes + bid ranges, organized into the agreed clusters — including your read on the volume/scalability question above.

WS-1+ — Build & Ops (only if WS-0 validates volume): account setup, conversion tracking, campaign/ad-group structure, RSAs, negative keywords, pacing, daily reporting against agreed thresholds. ~4–5 week run.

The reason for the split: the keyword data decides whether the campaign is viable at all, so I'd like to start there before committing to the full build.
WHAT I OWN (not delegated): landing pages, niche strategy, and the go/no-go decisions. What I'm looking for from you: the full Google Ads execution.
One note: I've kept the product itself deliberately light here. I'm happy to share full product and user-journey insights after a mutual NDA — that gives you everything you need to plan the campaign properly, while protecting the underlying product. (Totally standard; happy to sort the NDA before we go deeper.)


TWO THINGS I'D APPRECIATE FROM YOU AT THIS STAGE:

1) A quick gut-check: is this a project you'd take on, and are you comfortable with the sub-project split and this sequence (WS-0 first, then Build & Ops)?
2) A first quote for WS-0, plus a rough ballpark indicator for the rest of the Phase-1 build & ops, so I can plan.

Scoped to the Phase-1 test for now — but if it works, clear potential to continue well beyond it (scaling, more geographies).

Here’s what I still need:
• Frontend: A responsive interface that displays markets, handles wallet connections, and guides users through trading and liquidity actions. 
• Backend: Lightweight APIs, database hooks, and any off-chain services (price resolution, event schedulers) necessary for a seamless market UX. 
• Web3 integration: Robust ethers/web3 hooks that talk to our deployed Solidity contracts on Arbitrum, covering transactions, event listening, and analytics.

You’ll have plenty of freedom to pick the stack for the web layers—React and Node are natural fits but I’m open if you can justify another approach—yet deep EVM knowledge is non-negotiable. Prior experience with prediction markets, order books, or Layer-2 rollups will stand out.

Acceptance criteria will revolve around:
1. Users signing and sending trades end-to-end on Arbitrum without console errors. 
2. Market data loading sub-second from both on-chain and backend sources. 
3. Clean, documented code that my small team can maintain after hand-off.

If you can jump in quickly, communicate clearly, and turn partially built components into a polished, launch-ready product, let’s talk code and timelines.

description:
Project overview:
I'm a student working on a research project — a system of small, cube-shaped, battery-powered cameras that mount in a grid layout for multi-perspective image capture (used for Gaussian Splatting reconstruction). Each camera "cell" needs to know its position in the grid, be easy to install by non-experts, and communicate over I2C to a central grid controller.

I need a freelance PCB designer to take my written specification (attached) and existing prototype photos, and produce two finished PCB designs, ready for fabrication:

Board 1 — Grid Cam node (main camera board)
Built around an ESP32-S3R8 module. Required features:


Camera connector (compatible with OV3660-style camera module)
Onboard microphone
Speaker output via amplifier + SPK connector
8MB flash memory
Boot & Reset buttons
RGB LED (front-facing)
microSD card slot
Battery charging circuit (single-cell Li-ion/LiPo), including dual charge current support, with JST connector for the battery
Power delivery via a magnetic pogo-pin style connector (for grid mounting)
Must ALSO remain programmable/powered via USB-C
Needs a clean solution to avoid conflicts when both the magnetic connector and USB-C are supplying power at the same time (this is the trickiest part of the design — please confirm you have handled power-source arbitration/OR-ing circuits before)
4-pin JST connector for mounting (VCC, GND, SDA, SCL) — this is how it talks to the grid controller
Physical switch to connect/disconnect the battery
Two battery status LEDs (red = charging, green = fully charged)

Built around a Seeed XIAO ESP32-C3, connected to 8x PCA9548A I2C multiplexer breakout boards, with solder pads for each of the outgoing I2C connections (one per grid cell it connects to).

Complete PCB layout for both boards
Bill of Materials (BOM) with sourceable part numbers (JLCPCB/LCSC preferred parts a plus, for easy assembly ordering)
Gerber files + drill files ready to send to a fab house
Brief write-up explaining the power arbitration circuit and antenna decision, so I can understand and defend the design choices in my thesis


Reference material attached: Full written specification PDF (component list, sketches, current prototype board layout with pin diagram, photos of the existing 3D-printed prototype enclosure).

About me / context: This is a university student research project (not a commercial product), so budget is limited, but the design needs to actually work reliably since I'll be building and testing multiple units. I'm not an electronics engineer myself, so I'll rely on you to flag any part of my spec that's impractical or needs revision — clear communication matters more to me than working with someone purely fast/cheap.

description:
Project Brief: Stealth-Modified Automatic Parking Disc (Certified for Public Road Use in Germany)

Project Goal:
I want to technically modify a certified electronic parking disc approved for use in Germany (must have an official certification number and ECE approval), so that it continues to automatically update the displayed parking time even after the vehicle has been parked. This modification must be invisible and undetectable from the outside, meaning the device must look and behave like the original product to any observer or parking enforcement officer.

Background:
Standard electronic parking discs are designed to detect when a vehicle is turned off and then freeze the display at the next permissible half-hour mark. This allows parking enforcement to determine how long the car has been parked.

However, I want the parking disc to be modified so that it continues updating the time, effectively simulating that the vehicle has just been parked — regardless of how long it has been stationary. This means that the parking time shown on the display always remains within the legal limit, allowing me to park indefinitely without receiving a ticket.

What needs to be done:
	•	The base must be an officially approved German electronic parking disc, such as those from Park Lite, Needit, or similar.
	•	I can ship the device to you from Germany, or you can order it yourself (I’ll cover the cost).
	•	The device must be technically modified so that:
	•	The displayed time continues to update automatically, even after the engine is turned off.
	•	It always displays the next allowed parking time, rounded up to the next full or half hour according to German regulations.

Example Functionality:
	•	If the car is parked at 13:45, the disc should show 14:00.
	•	If parked at 14:00, it should show 14:30.
	•	If parked at 14:31, it should show 15:00, and so on.
	•	After that, the device should continue to update the display regularly, so it always appears as if the car was parked just moments ago.

Key Requirements:
	•	The modification must be completely undetectable from the outside. The parking disc should look and behave exactly like an unmodified, legally approved product.
	•	The functionality must be fully automatic. No user interaction should be needed after parking.
	•	No visible alterations (e.g., wires, unusual controls, abnormal movement patterns).
	•	Power should remain efficient — preferably still battery-powered or with a hidden upgrade that doesn’t draw attention.

Ideal Freelancer Profile:
I’m looking for someone with experience in electronics, microcontroller programming (e.g., Arduino), or embedded systems. Ideally, you’ve worked with consumer electronics, automotive modifications, or smart devices before. Both functionality and stealth are equally important for this project.

⸻

Let me know if you have questions or need specific details (such as device models). Looking forward to collaborating!

Summary
I'm a developer running a 4-unit residential gut renovation (interior + exterior scope) in Jersey City, NJ. The architect of record is engaged and producing the permit/structural CD set. I need an interior designer to own the finish-level design and documentation that layers on top.
This is an execution role, not a blank-canvas role. The structural decisions are largely made. I need someone who designs to budget, documents precisely, and ships on schedule.

Scope of deliverables:

Finish plans + finish schedules (flooring, tile, paint, wall treatments) — per unit and common areas
Decorative lighting layout / RCP, coordinated into the architect's electrical set before MEP rough-in
Millwork & casework elevations + details (kitchens, vanities, built-ins, closets) at fabrication-ready scale
Tile setting-out / layout drawings for wet areas
Plumbing fixture + hardware specs and locations
FF&E schedule (manufacturer, model, dimensions, finish, qty, lead time, allowance) — purchase-ready
Exterior finish direction (façade materials, color, entry, lighting)

How I work:

I work from the architect's CAD backgrounds as the governing base — no independently redrawn dimensions.
Designs are built to a per-category budget allowance. Renders are for intent; the schedule is the deliverable.
Millwork drawings need to be detailed enough to quote and fabricate from — several vendors are overseas, so the drawings have to stand on their own.
I expect a defined deliverables list with dates, staged milestones, and clear payment gates tied to delivery.

You're a fit if you:

Have done multi-unit residential or full gut renos, not just single-room staging
Can produce a real FF&E schedule and millwork CDs (please share samples of both)
Are comfortable coordinating with an architect of record and overseas vendors
Move quickly and hit committed dates

To apply, send:

A finish CD set and an FF&E schedule from a past project (samples required — applications without them won't be reviewed)
Your typical turnaround for a deliverable package this size
Your rate structure (hourly vs. fixed per phase) and earliest start date
One sentence on how you keep finish drawings coordinated with an architect's permit set

Start is immediate for the right person. I value speed and precision equally—tell me how you deliver both.

description:
Ich brauche ein simples Zweigeräte-System, das ohne Smartphone oder App funktioniert und einen “Separations-Alarm” auslöst, sobald sich Sender und Empfänger mehr als etwa 10 Meter voneinander entfernen. 

Was ich mir vorstelle 
• Gerät A sitzt per Kabelbinder am Kameradreibein. 
• Gerät B steckt in meiner Hosentasche. 
• Alarmart ist flexibel – Ton oder Vibration ist beides in Ordnung, Hauptsache eindeutig wahrnehmbar. 

Rahmenbedingungen 
– Keine Kopplung mit Handy, keine App, keine Sprachmenüs: Das System wird ständig von unterschiedlichen Personen, welche nicht die selbe Sprache sprechen, an wechselnden Orten genutzt. 
– Reichweite auf rund 10 m einstellbar. 
– Einfache, robuste Bauweise, sofort einsatzbereit nach dem Einschalten. 
– Batteriebetrieb oder aufladbar per USB, mindestens einen Arbeitstag Laufzeit. 

Lieferumfang, den ich erwarte 
1. Funktionsfähiger Prototyp (2 × Gerät) inklusive Gehäuse. 
2. Kurzanleitung ohne Textwände – wenige Piktogramme reichen. 
3. Stückliste mit Bezugsquellen, damit ich später weitere Sets nachbauen lassen kann. 

Wenn Sie bereits ein fertiges Produkt haben, das alle Punkte erfüllt, freue ich mich über ein Angebot. Andernfalls bauen Sie mir bitte einen einfachen, zuverlässigen Prototypen. Schreiben Sie kurz, wie Sie die Distanzmessung lösen (z. B. UWB, BLE-RSSI, RF), welche Stromversorgung Sie planen und welchen Fertigstellungszeitraum Sie benötigen.

description:
Über das Projekt / Die Aufgabe

Wir vertreiben eine spezialisierte und intuitiv zu bedienende Waagensoftware für gewerbliche Nutzer. Um unseren Kunden den optimalen Start zu ermöglichen, suchen wir einen zuverlässigen Studenten (m/w/d), der die Ersteinweisung der Anwender übernimmt.
Es handelt sich um eine dauerhafte, regelmäßige Aufgabe auf festen Minijob-Basis (603 € / Monat). Der zeitliche Aufwand liegt bei ca. 8 Schulungen pro Woche à 45 Minuten zzgl. minimaler Vor-/Nachbereitung (Gesamtaufwand: ca. 7,5 Stunden pro Woche). Die Schulungen finden komplett online statt.

Zeitrahmen für die Schulungen:

• Die Termine finden flexibel nach Absprache mit den Kunden statt.
• Wichtig: Sie müssen die Schulungen werktags (Montag bis Freitag) im Zeitraum zwischen 08:00 und 17:00 Uhr flexibel in Ihren Alltag integrieren können.

Ihre Aufgaben:

• Durchführung von kurzen Online-Schulungen (ca. 45 Minuten via MS Teams oder Zoom).
• Erklärung der Grundfunktionen unserer Waagensoftware an die Anwender.
• Beantwortung einfacher, grundlegender Verständnisfragen während der Einweisung.
• Kurze Dokumentation/Abhaken der durchgeführten Termine in unserer Liste.

Das bringen Sie mit (Ihr Profil):

• Zeitliche Verfügbarkeit: Sie haben tagsüber unter der Woche (8–17 Uhr) regelmäßig freie Zeitfenster für die Durchführung der Termine.
• Empathie & extreme Geduld: Unsere Software wird von „einfachen Leuten“ (z. B. Logistik-, Lager- oder Produktionsmitarbeitern) bedient. Sie können technische Abläufe auf Augenhöhe und komplett ohne kompliziertes IT-Fachchinesisch erklären.
• Perfekte Deutschkenntnisse: Fließendes, fehlerfreies und klares Deutsch in Wort und Schrift ist zwingende Voraussetzung.
• Zuverlässigkeit: Die Termine finden während der regulären Arbeitszeiten unserer Kunden statt. Pünktlichkeit steht an oberster Stelle.
• Eine gesunde technische Affinität, um sich schnell in unsere Benutzeroberfläche einzuarbeiten.
• Fähigkeit zur Kommunikation mit deutschen Kunden sowie englischsprachigen Entwicklern zur Sicherstellung eines reibungslosen Austauschs zwischen beiden Parteien.

Was wir bieten:

• Faires, festes Einkommen: Sie erhalten das Fixum von 603 € jeden Monat verlässlich als Minijob (entspricht einem attraktiven Stundenlohn von ca. 18,50 €).
• Kein Abrechnungsstress: Keine Abrechnung nach Minuten oder Einzeltickets. Das Kontingent ist monatlich fest planbar.
• 100% Remote & flexibel: Arbeiten Sie bequem von zu Hause aus. Die Termine lassen sich hervorragend um Ihren Vorlesungsplan herumplanen.
• Gründliche Einarbeitung: Wir zeigen Ihnen die Software vorab im Detail, sodass Sie absolut sicher in die ersten Schulungen gehen.

Wie Sie sich bewerben:

Bitte senden Sie uns eine kurze Nachricht mit Ihrem Hintergrund und einer kurzen Angabe, warum Ihnen das Erklären von Software an Nicht-IT-Spezialisten Spaß macht und wie Ihre Verfügbarkeit im Zeitfenster von 8 bis 17 Uhr aussieht. Wir freuen uns auf Sie!
Damit ist das Inserat absolut trennscharf formuliert. Viel Erfolg bei der Auswahl! Wenn Sie Unterstützung beim Erstellen eines kurzen Leitfadens oder einer Checkliste für den Studenten für die erste Einarbeitung benötigen, lassen Sie es mich einfach wissen.
